title: caesalpinia standleyi
intro: Caesalpinia standleyi, also known as the Standley's bird's claw or bird's claw caesalpinia, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family. It is a shrub native to Mexico and Central America, known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine. The plant contains compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, providing health benefits such as reducing inflammation and protecting against oxidative stress. Caesalpinia standleyi can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, or infusions, and is also used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever and respiratory issues.
